candidates.niche_name: Learned Token-Choice Routing for Sparse Mixture-of-Attention Heads
candidates.mechanism: MoSA routes distinct tokens to each attention head via expert-choice routing, and a learned router computes a distribution over heads-as-experts that assigns each input token to a small subset, which produces content-based sparse attention with token-level load balancing.
candidates.transfer: The expert-choice token-selection mechanism from A is transferred to B's learned router-over-experts framework so per-head attention experts are populated by a learned token-to-expert distribution.
candidates.open_problem: Does replacing MoSA's expert-choice head routing with a learned token-choice router over attention-head experts improve quality-vs-sparsity trade-offs without incurring token-dropping load imbalance?
candidates.primary_quote: MoSA selects distinct tokens for each head with expert choice routing

candidates.niche_name: Ultrasensitive Sigmoidal Gating for Specific Mixture-of-Experts Routing
candidates.mechanism: A learned router computes a sigmoidal (ultrasensitive) gating distribution that routes input tokens to experts, where sharp thresholding rather than cooperative expert co-activation produces input specificity. This induces a regime in which gate steepness regulates how decisively tokens are assigned, transforming soft mixtures into ultrasensitive switch-like expert selection.
candidates.transfer: The ultrasensitivity-via-sigmoidal-binding principle from IRF–DNA regulation transfers to A as a steepness-controlled routing mechanism that distinguishes sharp single-expert switching from cooperative multi-expert blending.
candidates.open_problem: Does increasing the sigmoidal steepness of an MoE router's gating function produce ultrasensitive token-to-expert specialization that outperforms cooperative soft routing on specificity-sensitive tasks?
candidates.primary_quote: A learned router computes a distribution over these experts, and assigns input tokens to a small subset.

candidates.niche_name: Expert-Choice Routing Models of Ultrasensitive Transcription-Factor Specificity
candidates.mechanism: Content-based expert-choice routing selects distinct tokens per attention head (as in MoSA), a mechanism repurposed to model how sigmoidal IRF–DNA binding routes transcriptional inputs to produce promoter-specific ultrasensitive activation rather than relying on IRF-NFκB cooperativity.
candidates.transfer: The per-head expert-choice sparse selection mechanism from A transfers to model the gene-specific input selection that ultrasensitive IRF–DNA binding performs in B.
candidates.open_problem: Can a learned expert-choice routing model predict which promoters acquire ultrasensitive specificity from sigmoidal IRF–DNA binding versus cooperative IRF-NFκB regulation?
candidates.primary_quote: MoSA selects distinct tokens for each head with expert choice routing
